text: Time is a limited resource that is irreversible and non-renewable. It is more than ever the factor which determines whether the consequences of climate change, energy conflicts, economic development, poverty and insecurity will be favourable or catastrophic. At the same time, an enormous amount of time is lost on a daily basis, time that belongs to others and to nature. These other resources are essential for combating violence and building sustainable security and for the well-being of all. It is therefore high time for us to rethink our relation to time and to develop a temporament that is capable of adapting to the urgent challenges of our time. This article begins by providing a definition of time and an overview of the gaps in relation to time that need to be addressed in order to ensure an adequate temporament for conflict transformation and then presents the parameters of a more time-efficient way of working in the context of peacebuilding, taking into account recent empirical research.
